body_excerpt: {"credits": [], "cve_id": "CVE-2025-13609", "cvss": {"score": 8.2, "vector_string": "CVSS:3.1/AV:N/AC:L/PR:H/UI:N/S:C/C:L/I:H/A:L"}, "cvss_severities": {"cvss_v3": {"score": 8.2, "vector_string": "CVSS:3.1/AV:N/AC:L/PR:H/UI:N/S:C/C:L/I:H/A:L"}, "cvss_v4": {"score": 0.0, "vector_string": null}}, "cwes": [{"cwe_id": "CWE-694", "name": "Use of Multiple Resources with Duplicate Identifier"}], "description": "A vulnerability has been identified in keylime where an attacker can exploit this flaw by registering a new agent using a different Trusted Platform Module (TPM) device but claiming an existing agent's unique identifier (UUID). title: Keylime allows users to register new agents by recycling existing UUIDs when using different TPM devices
